Hey guys, welcome back to module four. So by this point in the program, you should have crafted your segmentation and confirmation sequence. And in doing so, we've brought your prospect to a point where they feel verified and validated in their current reality, which includes that moment of highest tension we spoke about, as well as their aspirations. And we've given them a sense of renewed optimism, as well as the exclusive empowerment to re-pursue it. But while we have them excited, committed, and hopeful for that outcome, it's now time for us to solidify why those outcomes are achievable, to breathe a new sense of hope into the situation. And that's where the pre-launch really comes into play. It's really time for us to hone in on this main goal. It's to reward your prospect's excitement by showing them why those outcomes are achievable. And whereas the segmentation and confirmation sequence got them through the door and onto the journey, our major job here is to construct the arguments that fully liberate them from past failures and give them not just hope and commitment, but steadfast belief that the outcomes are possible. So here is what we are going to be coaching in the pre-launch sequence. So the first thing is going to be complete clarity over past failures and why the old way wasn't working for them. And in doing so, there's going to be this relinquishment of self-blame and judgment over those failures. That's one thing we really need to do. We need to release them from all that blame, all that self-doubt, because those are going to be things that create resistance later on. So this is going to be about forgiving that, relinquishing that self-blame, so that they're going to be empowered to act later. And the biggest aspect of the pre-launch is going to be after we dissolve the relinquishment and the self-blame, it's going to be all about giving them a new set of empowering and action oriented beliefs and understandings and attitudes. And this is going to span several categories. We need to get them on board with new beliefs about the opportunity and outcome they're trying to achieve and about the problem itself. It's not just enough to say it wasn't your fault. You need to say why. Why wasn't it their fault? And that's something that most marketers stop, fall short of. They'll say it's not your fault that X, Y, Z, but without qualifying why it wasn't their fault, without being hyper-specific about why it wasn't their fault, that understanding, that deeper attitude, that deeper belief, it just doesn't take root. We're also going to instill these new beliefs about the more empowering way to achieve it, which later on your product or your program is going to be uniquely qualified to serve, as well as creating new beliefs about their abilities and capacities to use that new way, and your ability to lead them through it. And finally, you do want to create those beliefs and understandings about your competitors as well as the commonly held conventions of your industry. When you set up your arguments like this, when you really give this full spectrum set of beliefs, what you're doing in turn is giving your prospect the gift of a more favorable and treatable diagnosis. We spoke about this a bit before when we were looking at the levels of awareness and tapping into symptomatically aware and then re-diagnosing it. A more favorable diagnosis, but one they need to act on, is a very powerful trigger, and one that we're really setting up here by layering in these beliefs. Because urgency, urgency doesn't come from despair. Urgency comes from a small window of hope and the opportunity to sneak in before it closes. Despair is essentially resignation. Well-grounded hope, hope that is grounded in new empowering beliefs and opportunity is what creates the energy to fuel action. So it's our job, that is our job. We need to ground our prospect's hope into fertile soil of new empowering beliefs that give them the chance to turn inspiration into action, to activate it, to activate it and really fuel them on that journey and get them to seize that opportunity while it's there. Because once you've given them that new diagnosis, there's a responsibility. You've given them clarity, you've set those new beliefs and it is up to them at this point to act on it. And essentially what we're doing is we're amplifying our prospect's responsibility, their ability to respond. And we've done that by bringing the clarity and closure over past failures. By elucidating those shortcomings, by pointing out those commonly accepted but non-serving methods and mindsets, which I call the sacred cows with sour milk. These are the things they never think to second guess because it just seems so natural and so set in stone. But once you release them from it, once you slaughter those sacred cows and give them the energy to pursue a new way, it becomes a responsibility. It increases their ability to respond. The other thing we're doing to increase that responsibility is we're indoctrinating them into that new set of empowering beliefs which creates that renewed optimism and motivation. It's kind of seeing that light and just wanting to move towards it, really use that, leverage these insights that you and not your competitors have. So that's a very powerful thing that amplifies their response ability. And finally, it's that whole gift of that expert diagnosis which earns you the exclusive right to prescribe and earns your prospect the ability to respond, the ability and the responsibility to act on it. So those are the major points here. And what we're really tapping into in the pre-launch, once we've accomplished it, we've set up this whole dynamic of it's not your fault until it is. That is the enduring point that we want to accomplish by the end of this sequence. Once you accurately and successfully diagnose and prescribe in an indisputable accepted way, your prospect can no longer defer responsibility. So this is what we're going for. We've cleared them of past mistakes, we've shown them a new path towards that result and outcome and it is now their responsibility to follow it. And that's something we have the duty to keep them accountable to later on. So we're going to get into some theme plates and exploratory questions in just a few minutes, but I just want to take an extra minute or so to talk about sacred cows with sour milk. This is one of the main things that I focus on when developing which beliefs are going to really have the most impact. Which ones I need to be focusing on and which ones are going to, which ones are prospect is most ready and willing to hear and accept. So that's really something that I want to take an extra second to describe, but essentially the sacred cows with sour milk are those conventional, commonly held, yet oppressive beliefs that derail the progress of your market. So these are the beliefs that may overwhelm or frighten or restrict them. They're oppressive and they're non-serving, yet at the same time these are the ones that are so ingrained and reinforced that they don't even think to challenge them even when they don't serve. And that's why if you do, you're building a ton of goodwill and trust with your audience because you're giving them the gift of removing or reshifting these sacred cows that just aren't serving. So your job as a conversion coach is to deconstruct it and shift it in a stimulating and logically satisfying way. Of course it has to make sense, you know, but the fact that it's in your audience's best interest to be open to a new, better, more empowering and encouraging alternative is what sets the stage of receptivity for you to do that. So this is where the challenging aspect of coaching comes into play. You need to be willing to challenge your market's assumptions, your market's sacred cows, and to do so with courage and conviction. So a few examples of sacred cows could be, I can't build an online business until I've learned how to use WordPress. That probably held me back more than anything about eight years ago is I thought I had to learn code. I was going on Udemy and, you know, all sorts of resources to try to learn how to code because I thought that was a prerequisite for owning an online business and I probably struggled with that sacred cow for about two years until I can't remember who slaughtered it for me, but I'm sure I invested in their program. Another one that I struggled with is I can't play guitar until I learn how to read sheet music or chords or tabs and I think I invested in a few online courses that said you don't have to do that. You know, there were other methods of learning how to do it, so they slaughtered that sacred cow for me and earned a lot of trust and excitement and more importantly, I wanted to buy in to that new belief because the sacred cow wasn't serving me. It was oppressive and holding me back from an outcome I really wanted to have. Another one in the online business world is in order to have a six-figure business, I need a massive list. You know, I hear that one all the time. People think that their income in their online business is going to be totally dependent and correlated with the size of their list, you know, instead of just building a high converting funnel, you know, something that can convert at five, eight, ten percent and then being able to afford to invest in the traffic to put in a dollar in ads and get five or ten dollars back, you know, you don't need a massive list to do that. Another one in languages, I can't learn how to speak French because I'm terrible at memorizing verbs. You know, that might be a sacred cow that's holding back your prospects from taking that leap with you. So these are things we want to deconstruct. We want to help them blast through these widely accepted and non-serving beliefs which in turn really liberate them. It frees them from that perception and it's really a gift. You know, when we talk about education and value, we're not just talking about how to information. You know, sometimes being valuable and educational means educating people over their past failures, you know, giving value through encouraging them to get through those things that have been insidiously holding them back, slaughtering those sacred cows with them because you're an expert who can deconstruct it, you know, and could lead them to a more favorable place. So education isn't just how to. It's why X happened and why it doesn't have to happen like that anymore. That is one of the more powerful and not leveraged forms of online education that I really encourage you to use in your pre-launch. And this all culminates, you know, at the end of the pre-launch, it culminates with this belief that the outcome can happen. So they're no longer just committed to it. They believe that that outcome can happen. And there's even this growing sense of inevitability around it in your presence, you know, even before you've shown them the opportunity, just in your presence as an authority of someone who's elucidated all these sacred cows and earned that trust and got them encouraged, there's this sense that in your presence, these outcomes are inevitable. And that's what we've set up. So this is everything we're doing in the pre-launch on a conceptual level. It's really time to take all of these concepts and ground it into the actual execution. So I want us to take a little breather here. You could re-watch this if you feel like you need to steep in these concepts a little more. But when you're ready, meet me in the workbook, meet me in the theme plates, and you'll see exactly the questions we use to draw out and extract the content for the pre-launch subsequence. And then the theme plates that allow us to really kind of have the containers to make them active in an efficient way. So that's it. One final note, if you're doing a webinar, for example, the pre-launch is actually going to be the first 30 minutes of your webinar before you introduce your offer, which would be considered at the launch. So generally in those first 30 minutes, that's where you're really creating new beliefs about the opportunity or maneuvering, you're reframing, you're giving insights, and you're giving proper diagnosis. So this will happen on the webinar itself, but it's still going to be extremely beneficial for you to just go through these practices and exercises and make sure that you're dialing in these points when creating your webinar. And then if you're doing a three-part video series workshop challenge, then you're going to be sending out pre-launch emails to point to that asset. So you're essentially going to be, the main job of the emails are going to be to sell the asset, to draw curiosity around the video or the challenge or whatever format you're using for that. So those emails are going to be shorter, more of a tease, and you're going to see the theme plates we use for those. The email itself won't have to do the heavy lifting, but they're still going to be vitally important to get people to consume the pre-launch content, whether it is in a Facebook group, whether it's a video, a challenge, whatever you're doing, you're going to have to use those emails to encourage consumption. But if you are going to be following this course in full and doing the minimum viable launch where you're not recording any videos, you're not doing any challenges, you're doing an email-only funnel, then we're also going to show you a theme plate that allows you the container to create those shifts all within the email itself. So it is going to be longer than the emails you're going to be using if you're just selling the asset like a video, but there is a way to do it in email-only, and you're going to get that theme plate in a minute. So I'm going to take a deep breath, get a drink, and I will meet you back in the workbook and the theme plates so that we can wrap up the pre-launch and really get it done in a powerful way. So that's it, I'll see you in the next video.
